HEMATOLOGY: THROMBOEMBOLIC DISEASE</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4899445E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>A. VTE Epidemiology &amp; Risk Factors</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3AE09D78" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="58" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Epidemiology</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5605DDD5" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="58" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Venous Thromboembolism (VTE) consists of 2/3 Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and 1/3 Pulmonary Embolism (PE).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3A1C1B08" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="58" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Diagnostic Overlap:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> With proximal DVT, 40% also have a PE. 70% of patients diagnosed with a PE also have a DVT if investigated.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6345D5E6" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="58" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Mortality:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> 6% for DVT (typically due to progression or comorbidities) and 12% for PE.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="092BCA26" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="58" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Virchow's Triad &amp; Expanded Risk Factors</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6FF6D55D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="58" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Triad:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Stasis, Hypercoagulability, Endothelial injury.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6B644E83" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="58" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Expanded Risks:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Male gender, Family history of VTE (thrombophilia), Obesity (Weight &lt; 50 kg is typically associated with cancer, not standard VTE risk), Malignancy (specifically myeloproliferative disorders, stage 4, lung, ovary, brain), Pregnancy/postpartum, and COVID-19.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0224F001" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>B. Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) Diagnosis &amp; Workup</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5E1805F1" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Wells Criteria for DVT (Pretest Probability)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5AA8D581" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Scoring (1 point each):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Active cancer (treatment within last 6 mo/palliative), Paralysis/paresis/immobilization, Bedridden &gt;3 days or major surgery within 4 wks, Localized tenderness along deep veins, Entire leg swollen, Calf swelling &gt;3 cm in symptomatic leg, Pitting edema greater in symptomatic leg, Collateral superficial veins.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1A139660" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Alternative Diagnosis (-2 points):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Subtract 2 points if an alternative diagnosis (e.g., sprained ankle) is more likely.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7C7C928D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Interpretation:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> High Risk = 3+, Intermediate = 1-2, Low = 0.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="520F1578" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>D-Dimer (ELISA)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="27B78C50" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Excellent test to rule </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>out</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> disease (95% Negative Predictive Value), poor test to rule </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>in</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="478EAC3C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Standard cutoff is &lt; 500 mcg/L. </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Age Nuance:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> False positives increase with age. Consider utilizing an age-adjusted cutoff of [Age x 10] for patients &gt; 50 years old.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4772451A" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Algorithm:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Normal D-dimer + Low Wells Score = DVT excluded.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="05D1BEFB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Venous Compression Ultrasound</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5A985010" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>The test of choice for Moderate/High Wells Score (skip the D-dimer entirely).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="20446E73" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Has a 94% Positive Predictive Value for symptomatic proximal thrombosis.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7C4DF62F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Limitations:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Can miss isolated pelvic clots. Tumors and abscesses can cause false positives. Cannot distinguish new vs. old clot.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="55EA630E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Follow-up:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> If the ultrasound is negative but clinical suspicion remains high, repeat the ultrasound in 5-7 days.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1BE70534" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Post-DVT Malignancy &amp; Thrombophilia Screening</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="08BF4BB3" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Malignancy (SOR A): Routine age-appropriate cancer screening only. Perform a complete H&amp;P (including rectal/pelvic), CBC, LFTs, CXR, and stool guaiac. Aggressive "hunting" for occult cancer is not cost-effective.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2DC7C129" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Thrombophilia (SOR A): Do </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>not</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> routinely screen. Screen strictly if: initial clot occurs prior to age 50, family history of VTE, recurrent clot, unusual vascular beds (e.g., gut/brain), or warfarin-induced skin necrosis.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6349FF09" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="252" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Testing Protocol:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Test Protein C, Protein S, Fibrinogen, Antithrombin III, Factor V Leiden, Lupus anticoagulant, Anticardiolipin antibody, and Prothrombin 20210. </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Pearl:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Testing must ideally occur </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>before</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> starting anticoagulants, as active clots and medications render Protein C/S and ATIII testing inaccurate.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="28C9DE81" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>C. Pulmonary Embolism (PE) Diagnosis</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6369B35B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="17" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Wells Criteria for PE</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6912A89C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="17" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Scoring:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Symptoms of DVT (3), Other Dx less likely (3), HR &gt;100 (1.5), Immobilization/surgery in past 4 wks (1.5), Previous DVT/PE (1.5), Hemoptysis (1), Malignancy (1).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0BDB60DC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="17" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Interpretation:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> High Risk &gt; 6, Intermediate = 2-6, Low &lt; 2.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7A1F98E2" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="17" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Pulse Oximetry:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> A normal SpO2 strictly rules out a </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>massive</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> PE, but does not rule out standard PE. EKG/CXR are nonspecific.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="29390273" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="17" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Diagnostic Imaging Modalities</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0B1FC26F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="17" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Computed Tomography Pulmonary Angiography (CTPA): The test of choice for high/moderate pretest probability, or a low probability with a positive D-dimer. Preferred over traditional pulmonary angiography (which carries 2% mortality and 5% morbidity).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5C72C8D5" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="17" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">V/Q Scanning: The imaging of choice </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>only</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> if CTPA is contraindicated (contrast allergy, renal disease &gt; Stage 3, or in pregnancy if only a high-dose full CT scanner is available). A strictly "normal" V/Q scan completely excludes a PE regardless of pretest probability.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3A8F692C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>D. VTE Treatment &amp; Pharmacology (ACCP Guidelines)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2AC29600" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Direct Oral Anticoagulants (DOACs)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="06014FC0" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Preferred over Warfarin for VTE treatment due to increased time in therapeutic window and lower major bleeding. Must dose-adjust for renal disease.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7B5D32BE" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>No Bridge Required:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Rivaroxaban (Xarelto) and Apixaban (Eliquis).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4170937E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>LMWH Bridge Required:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Dabigatran (Pradaxa) and Edoxaban (Savaysa).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="16D714FA" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Reversal Agents:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Specific antidotes, Charcoal, or Hemodialysis.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="544C8A3E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Cancer-Associated Thrombosis</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="17605B0C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>DOACs are recommended over Low-Molecular-Weight Heparin (LMWH) for general cancer patients.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="66918BC3" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Exception:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> For GI luminal cancers, LMWH is superior to DOACs due to bleeding risk.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="78AB9EB7" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Warfarin is strictly 3rd-line for cancer patients (higher recurrence and bleeding).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6AF4BB61" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Exceptions to Anticoagulation (Watchful Waiting)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="49C55D77" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Acute Isolated Distal DVT: Anticoagulation can be skipped if there are no severe symptoms and low risk for extension. Monitor with serial ultrasound for 2 weeks. </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Risk factors forcing treatment:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Positive D-dimer, clot &gt;5 cm, proximity to proximal veins, unprovoked clot, active cancer, or inpatient status (SOR A).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="339F875B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Sub-segmental PE: Clinical surveillance is recommended over anticoagulation if there is no proximal pulmonary artery involvement, no proximal DVT, and low risk for recurrent VTE.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0A1881D5" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Treatment Duration &amp; Setting</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="69397EB3" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Outpatient DOAC Criteria:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Stable, low bleeding risk, no renal insufficiency, and reliable for follow-up.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5DBE8D51" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Duration Guidelines:</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="724032AA" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Provoked (transient surgical/COVID risk): 3 months.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="23E4A696" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Unprovoked: 3+ months (long-term if bleeding risk is low/moderate; if stopping at 3 months, initiate Aspirin).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="15D781AA" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Recurrent DVT/PE: Indefinite.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="079620F1" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Physical Interventions</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="257A6A49" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Compression Stockings: No longer recommended. They show </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>no evidence of benefit</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> for preventing post-thrombotic syndrome or recurrent DVT (Hematology Choosing Wisely Campaign).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="644C36DA" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Bedrest: Early ambulation is specifically suggested instead of prolonged bedrest.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5AE13C35" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Catheter-Directed Thrombolysis: Adjunct to reduce clot burden/valve damage in iliac and proximal femoral DVT. Carries risk of intracranial bleeding.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0DCFB67E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="30" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Systemic Thrombolysis: Indicated for severe PE presenting with hypotension (SBP &lt; 90).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7498AF33" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>E. VTE Management in Pregnancy</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="208636E8" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Epidemiology &amp; Diagnosis</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="57F53407" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Risk increases 5-fold (1/1,600 pregnancies). 20-50% have an underlying thrombophilia. Risk is equal throughout all trimesters, but PE is highly common in the first week postpartum.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="74D9D0E6" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>D-dimer is useless (almost always positive).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="632FF805" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Imaging Hierarchy:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Perform CXR first. If normal \rightarrow V/Q scan. If abnormal \rightarrow Full dose CT scan (if low-dose CTPA is not available).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="424ACA62" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Pharmacology in Pregnancy &amp; Breastfeeding</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4A42E106" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Safe: Heparin and LMWH (do not cross placenta). LMWH is preferred due to less bleeding, osteoporosis, and HIT compared to Unfractionated Heparin (UFH). Safe for breastfeeding.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="02BF91B4" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Contraindicated: Warfarin (crosses placenta, causes harmful fetal effects). Avoid estrogen-containing contraceptives postpartum.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0A4556CC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Insufficient Data: DOACs.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="211F90A1" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Peri-Delivery Management (SOR B)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="52FC23A7" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Epidural Rule:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Must wait 12 hours after the last dose of LMWH before epidural anesthesia (SOR C).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2DF0B0F2" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Convert from LMWH to UFH for the last month of pregnancy to allow tighter procedural control. Use compression stockings during the peripartum gap.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="05C10C09" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Restarting Anticoagulation:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> 4-6 hours post-vaginal delivery; 6-12 hours post-C-section.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="59F759C2" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Postpartum Bridging:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> If bridging to Warfarin postpartum, use LMWH for 5 days until INR is 2-3 for 2 consecutive days to avoid warfarin-induced skin necrosis (due to transient anti-Protein C effect).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="013B52EE" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="234" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>COVID-19:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Pregnant patients hospitalized for COVID require VTE prophylaxis inpatient and for 2 weeks post-discharge.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5BE913B4" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><pict ns2:anchorId="54234B12"><ns3:rect id="_x0000_i1050" style="width:0;height:1.5pt" ns4:hralign="center" ns4:hrstd="t" ns4:hr="t" fillcolor="#a0a0a0" stroked="f" /></pict></r></p><p ns2:paraId="17A5D6C8" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00586D48" rsidP="00586D48"><pPr><pStyle val="Style1" /></pPr><r><t>26. HEMATOLOGY: ANEMIAS &amp; HEMOGLOBINOPATHIES</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="502D754C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>A. Anemia Diagnostic Algorithms</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7AF9B8C0" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="220" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Kinetic Approach (Reticulocyte Index)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="59319D47" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="220" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Decreased Production (Retic &lt; 2):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Lack of substrate, marrow disorders, decreased trophic hormones, chronic illness.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5F6B8CA0" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="220" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Increased Loss (Retic &gt; 2):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Hemolysis (inherited/acquired) or active blood loss.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3BFEBC2E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="220" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Morphologic Approach (MCV Indices)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3685F353" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="220" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Macrocytic (MCV &gt;100): B12 and folate deficiencies, myelodysplasia, alcohol abuse, liver disease, hypothyroidism. Shows reticulocytosis (high RDW). </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Pharmacologic Pearl:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs) and Metformin are highly tested iatrogenic causes of B12 deficiency.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4BD47CFC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="220" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Microcytic (MCV &lt;80): Iron deficiency, lead toxicity, sideroblastic anemia, thalassemia.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="31CBB47A" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="220" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Normocytic: Acute blood loss, acute hemolysis, hypersplenism, anemia of chronic disease. </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Board Pearl:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> If the anemia is due to a chronic disease (e.g., Rheumatoid Arthritis), the treatment is exclusively addressing the underlying disease (DMARDs), not iron or B12 supplementation.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6ED7797E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>B. Iron Deficiency Anemia</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5B5CDA6C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Diagnosis &amp; Investigation</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1434409F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Lab Profile:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Low iron, High Total Iron Binding Capacity (TIBC), Low ferritin.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="339FD001" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Clinical Rule:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Iron deficiency is a symptom, not a final diagnosis. You must identify the source of loss (menstrual vs. occult GI malignancy).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="14FB91B4" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Treatment &amp; Recovery Timeline</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="121169FB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Low-Dose Iron: 15 mg of elemental iron (or alternate-day dosing) is effective and causes significantly fewer GI side effects (SOR C).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="411B6F84" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Response Timeline:</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5491F0AC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Reticulocyte count normalizes in 5-7 days. (If it fails to rise, you have not stopped the bleeding or they are not absorbing the iron).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7DCAC887" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Hematocrit corrects in 4-6 weeks.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="677BDF46" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="279" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Total Iron stores replenish in 4-6 months. </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Therapeutic Pearl:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Iron therapy must be continued for a full 6 months after blood counts normalize to refill marrow stores.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="16316091" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>C. Sickle Cell Disease (Hemoglobin SS)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7F524FDF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Diagnostic Classifications &amp; Clinical Hallmarks</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="13CF1CDE" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Hemoglobin S forms polymers when deoxygenated, deforming the cell.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0B93380F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Variants:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Homozygous SS is classic. Sickle SC has less severe overall disease but carries a much higher risk of retinal disease.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="29DA4EE6" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Manifestations:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Moderate-severe anemia (Hgb &lt;8) with high reticulocyte count.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="371190EB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Crises:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Vaso-occlusive (muscle, CVA, priapism), Aplastic (Parvovirus B19 marrow suppression), Acute Chest Syndrome (pneumonia + infarct), Splenic sequestration.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="37F14629" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Acute Pain Management (SOR B)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3E1D74FA" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Utilize IV narcotics for severe pain crises.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0B1D6BBF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Utilize NSAIDs for mild-to-moderate pain.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="02BB3876" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Prophylaxis &amp; Screening</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6371B193" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Penicillin Prophylaxis: Penicillin V PO 125 mg BID from age 3 months to 3 years. Increase to Pen V PO 250 mg BID from age 3 to 5 years. Continuation after age 5 is controversial.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1BB642C7" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Immunizations: PCV13 and 20, Menactra and Men-B (early administration), Hib, Hep B, Influenza.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3A2C85C3" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Stroke Screening (SOR A): Annual screening utilizing Transcranial Doppler Ultrasound to evaluate the risk of stroke is mandatory for patients aged 2 to 16 years.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="59146FE4" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Hydroxyurea Therapy (SOR B)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="001566CF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Mechanism:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> A disease-modifying agent that increases levels of Hemoglobin F (HbF), significantly reducing vaso-occlusive crises, acute chest syndrome, transfusions, and mortality.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5015B2AB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Indications:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Initiated at age 9-42 months, or in adults suffering  &gt; 3 crises in a 12-month period.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6B742F0C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="254" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Management &amp; Risks:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Requires strict contraceptive counseling (teratogenic, enters breast milk). Requires monitoring of CBC and reticulocyte count every 4 weeks due to risk of severe neutropenia and thrombocytopenia.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="633DE089" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><pict ns2:anchorId="0D52F0EA"><ns3:rect id="_x0000_i1051" style="width:0;height:1.5pt" ns4:hralign="center" ns4:hrstd="t" ns4:hr="t" fillcolor="#a0a0a0" stroked="f" /></pict></r></p><p ns2:paraId="57DBC74D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00586D48" rsidP="00586D48"><pPr><pStyle val="Style1" /></pPr><r><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>27. HEMATOLOGY: MALIGNANCIES &amp; COAGULATION DISORDERS</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2579C584" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>A. Multiple Myeloma</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4B9B2C0C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Epidemiology &amp; Presentation</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1AF5B30F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Represents 10% of all hematologic cancers. Risk factors: Age &gt;65, African American descent.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5EE31319" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Classic Tetrad:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Anemia (73%), Bone pain (58%), Elevated Creatinine (48%), Hypercalcemia (28%). Also presents with recurrent infections and weight loss.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="48609F51" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Diagnosis &amp; Primary Care Management</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3DCCBC38" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Diagnostics:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Monoclonal protein in serum/urine, bone lesions on skeletal survey, abnormal plasma cells on bone marrow biopsy. Order CBC with diff, LDH, beta-2 microglobulin, and immunoglobulins.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="725D895B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Primary Care Responsibilities:</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="096B602C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Initiate Bisphosphonate therapy immediately upon diagnosis.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7755320D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>DVT prevention utilizing a DOAC or LMWH.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1A6E1429" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Prophylactic antibiotics (TMP/SMX + Fluoroquinolones) for the first 3 months of treatment. Penicillin prophylaxis for recurrent pneumococcal infections.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0C08310E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Vaccinations: PCV, Hib, Influenza.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="314E967E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="89" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Avoid all nephrotoxic medications.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2EF0204A" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>B. Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (TTP)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4D13796B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Pathophysiology &amp; Causes</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7F25E318" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Usually idiopathic, but strongly associated with ADAMTS13 deficiency. ADAMTS13 is a protease that breaks down Von Willebrand Factor (VWF). Without it (via an inhibitory auto-antibody), long polymers of VWF attract and destroy platelets.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6B861F21" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Secondary Causes:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Shiga-toxin from </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>E. coli</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> 0157:H7, and Medications (specifically Clopidogrel, Ticlopidine, Quinine, Mitomycin, Tacrolimus).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1365284E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>The Classic Pentad</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2AC800CB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>It is clinically rare for all five to be present simultaneously:</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="55D33806" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Microangiopathic hemolytic anemia.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="23191493" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Thrombocytopenia (with purpura).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="14370F71" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Acute renal insufficiency (HUS variant).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1F5812D5" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Neurological symptoms (TTP variant).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="10D73260" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Fever.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2AB43ACF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Treatment</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5F40D7F0" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="109" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Stop offending medications. Plasma exchange therapy is curative.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="55A8E9A1" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>C. Leukemia &amp; Hemophilia</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4C179E5A" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Acute vs Chronic Leukemia</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4122CCDE" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Predominantly in children. Presents acutely with fever, bleeding, hepatosplenomegaly (75%), and lymphadenopathy.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6F1A493F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Acute Myelogenous Leukemia (AML):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Predominantly in adults. Presents with fever, fatigue, bleeding, and Rouleaux formation on smear.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5E78E59A" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Chronic Leukemias (CLL/CML):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Occur in adults. Patients are almost exclusively asymptomatic at diagnosis. Discovered incidentally via marked leukocytosis on a routine CBC. CLL features hepatosplenomegaly; CML features pure splenomegaly.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5F0D3E8D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Diagnostic Workup:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Repeat the CBC, order a peripheral smear, obtain flow cytometry, and refer to oncology.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1A259FDC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Hemophilia A &amp; B</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="30727F55" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>X-linked recessive bleeding disorders predominantly affecting males.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="338BB380" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Hemophilia A = Factor VIII deficiency. Hemophilia B = Factor IX deficiency (Christmas Disease).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="11720441" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="122" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Clinical Pearls:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> They become symptomatic within the first two years of life, but notably, only 50% experience bleeding with circumcision. Hemarthrosis (bleeding into joints) causes severe late joint destruction; joints are only preserved by early initiation of factor concentrate treatments.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5E19E16B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="008370D8" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr></p><p ns2:paraId="21E99B5E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing ns2:paraId="E8D72EE6" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="00CC0000" rsidRDefault="00CC0000"><pPr><shd val="clear" color="auto" fill="D6E4F7" /><spacing before="0" after="0" /><ind left="180" right="0" ns2:paraId="E4041A42" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="00CC0000" rsidRDefault="00CC0000"><pPr><shd val="clear" color="auto" fill="D6E4F7" /><spacing before="0" after="0" /><ind left="180" right="0" xml:space="preserve">  AAFP Poll Questions — Session 22: Fever &amp; Infectious Disease in Children</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="0" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="7F4F24" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  5 practice questions from the AAFP Board Prep presentation. Work each question before checking the answer key.</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  1.  A 3-year-old girl presents with 4-day history of fever without other symptoms. Ears, throat, lungs and abdominal exams are normal, but she appears lethargic. What should be your next step?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Treat fever and reevaluate her in 24 hours</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Order urinalysis with cultures</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Order an abdominal ultrasound</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Administer IV fluids</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 22</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  2.  A 16-year-old female patient is evaluated for a 4-day history of sore throat, nonproductive cough and rhinorrhea. She is afebrile, and exam reveals enlarged erythematous tonsils without exudate, and no lymphadenopathy. What is the next step?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Supportive care</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Rapid strep test</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Treat with oral penicillin for 10 days</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Treat with azithromycin for 5 days</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 22</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  3.  A 2-year-old child is brought to the emergency department with a barking cough, stridor that worsened tonight, and a temperature of 101º F. What is the treatment?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Nebulized albuterol</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Inhaled steroids</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Nebulized epinephrine and dexamethasone</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Humidified oxygen</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 22</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  4.  9-month-old child is admitted in January for cough, wheezing, feeding, and fever of 38ºC. CXR shows mild peribronchial cuffing. Which treatment is indicated?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Amoxicillin/clavulanate (Augmentin)</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Systemic corticosteroids</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Supplemental oxygen and fluids</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Nebulized ipratropium (Atrovent)</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 22</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  5.  A 2-year-old boy has very red cheeks and a fine rash but does not appear ill. He had a fever a couple of days ago. When can he return to day care?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial"
