/></pPr><r><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>28. PEDIATRICS: FEVER &amp; INFECTIOUS DISEASE</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="61A7F52F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>A. Fever in Infants (&lt; 90 Days)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0375FA86" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Definition &amp; General Rules</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6018901D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Fever is defined strictly as  &gt; 100.4^\circ\text{F} (38^\circ\text{C}) rectally.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="295D9389" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Fever does not cause brain damage, but can cause discomfort and seizures. It acts to fight infection.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="05F1B0EF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Infants &lt; 21 Days Old</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="241D8336" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Mandatory Protocol:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Admit to hospital for a full septic workup.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1B3F85E9" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Diagnostics:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> CBC with differential, blood cultures, chest x-ray (if respiratory symptoms), urinalysis and urine culture, lumbar puncture (CSF studies/culture), procalcitonin, CRP, and HSV studies.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6F010692" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Administrative Pearl:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Utilizing a newborn sepsis calculator can successfully decrease excessive antibiotic use.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="15660175" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Pharmacology (IV Antibiotics):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Ampicillin + Gentamicin, OR Ampicillin + Cefotaxime. Add Vancomycin if </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Streptococcus pneumoniae</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> meningitis is suspected. Add Acyclovir for HSV.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0552A712" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Black Box Pearl:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Strictly avoid Ceftriaxone in infants &lt; 1 month old due to the severe risk of kernicterus.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4EE00E8B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Infants 21 - 90 Days Old</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="049ABC66" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Ill-Appearing:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> If the Pediatric Assessment Triangle is abnormal (poor rousability/cry, abnormal airway sounds/retractions, poor capillary refill/mottling), treat identically to infants &lt; 21 days (Admit, full workup).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3F3A369F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Well-Appearing ("Step-by-Step" Approach):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Used to determine outpatient viability.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="374E59FD" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Step 1: Dipstick Urine. Is it positive for leukocytes? (If Yes \rightarrow High Risk/Admit).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="53DF0BC9" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Step 2: Procalcitonin. Is it &gt; 0.5 \text{ ng/ml}? (If Yes \rightarrow High Risk/Admit).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2ED405CC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Step 3: CRP or ANC. Is CRP &gt; 20 \text{ mg/L} or Absolute Neutrophil Count &gt; 10,000/\text{mm}^3? (If Yes \rightarrow High Risk/Admit).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="16EF2C8B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="42" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>If all three are NO \rightarrow Low Risk. Can be treated via community standards for close outpatient observation.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="708C8445" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>B. Febrile Seizures &amp; Upper Respiratory Infections</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="59BB389C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="246" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Febrile Seizures</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7E77A816" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="246" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Occur in 2-5% of children (most common at 9-20 months). 30-40% will have a recurrence. Not associated with brain damage.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4F785804" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="246" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Simple:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> &lt; 15 minutes, generalized, occurs only once in 24 hours. </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Rule:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> No neurologic evaluation or imaging is indicated for a first simple febrile seizure other than working up the source of the fever.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="56D56D64" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="246" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Complex:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> &gt; 15 minutes, focal, or recurs within 24 hours.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="54D26A4C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="246" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Vaccine Nuance:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> There is an increased risk of febrile seizures if the MMR vaccine is given at 16-23 months instead of the recommended 13-15 months.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2744BEB3" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="246" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Viral Upper Respiratory Infections</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1354B1BF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="246" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Contraindication:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Over-the-counter cough and cold medications are strictly NOT recommended for children under the age of 4 (they cause more harm than good).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3333A243" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="246" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Evidence-Based Treatment:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Honey (strictly for age &gt; 1 year), nasal saline irrigation, vapor rub (strictly for age &gt; 2 years), and analgesics. Do not use antibiotics.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5EE4AED1" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>C. Pediatric Infections &amp; Airway Emergencies</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="061376E6" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Urinary Tract Infections (UTI)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="33C50968" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">The most common serious bacterial infection in children &lt; 3 years old. Look for it when there is no obvious source of fever. 70-90% caused by </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>E. coli</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2906A8EF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Diagnosis:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Requires a urinalysis with pyuria AND a positive culture (&gt; 50,000 \text{ CFU/mL}). Specimen must be obtained via catheterization or suprapubic tap (bag urine is invalid for culture).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7F8B9D9B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Treatment:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Cefixime, Amoxicillin/Clavulanate, or TMP-SMX for 7-14 days.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2A2A39C6" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Imaging Follow-up:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Order a Renal Ultrasound if it is their first febrile UTI and they are &lt; 2 years old, OR if they have recurrent UTIs. Order a Voiding Cystourethrogram (VCUG) if the ultrasound shows hydronephrosis/scarring, or for recurrent febrile UTIs.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="30F71B26" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Pharyngitis (Group A Strep vs Viral)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="12D95FD1" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Viruses are the most common cause. Group A Beta-Hemolytic Strep (GABHS) causes 15%.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="383D94B1" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Modified Centor Criteria:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Fever &gt; 38^\circ\text{C} (+1), Absence of cough (+1), Swollen/tender anterior nodes (+1), Tonsillar swelling/exudate (+1). Age 3-14 (+1), Age 15-44 (0), Age  &gt; 45 (-1).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2D7E13E2" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Score 0-1: No testing.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="58CD86D0" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Score 2-3: Rapid antigen test. </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Pediatric Rule:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Always send a backup culture if the rapid test is negative.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5FB9BEDD" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Score 4-5: Treat empirically.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="73DC144D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Guideline Restriction:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> IDSA guidelines strongly recommend </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>against</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> testing for strep in children &lt; 3 years old; positive results in this age group represent colonization, not active infection.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5D03BD04" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Treatment &amp; Complications:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Penicillin or Amoxicillin for 10 days. Treatment prevents rheumatic fever but does </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>not</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> prevent post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="737723C8" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>PANDAS:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Pediatric Autoimmune Neuropsychiatric Disorders Associated with Streptococcal Infections. Suspect if a child presents with abrupt, severe psychiatric changes following a strep infection.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3CA4ADFC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Croup (Laryngotracheobronchitis)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="14B37FFB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Caused by Parainfluenza viruses. Features a "barky" seal-like cough, mild stridor (worse at night), and a "steeple sign" on x-ray.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="45417515" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Treatment Paradigm:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Give Dexamethasone 0.15 - 0.6 mg/kg IM or PO as a single dose for ALL severities of croup (reduces admission and ED stay).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7B35899B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>For moderate-to-severe croup (stridor at rest, retractions): Add nebulized racemic epinephrine and observe for at least 4 hours.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4FECFD7D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Contraindication:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Beta-agonist bronchodilators (Albuterol) are NOT effective.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="51C579C1" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Bronchiolitis</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1FDCB6AF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">Caused by RSV. Features rhinorrhea, cough, and </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>wheezing</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> in infants &lt; 2 years.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="71591BAE" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Treatment:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Strictly supportive (oxygen, acetaminophen, fluids, nebulized hypertonic saline).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0287E6B0" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Contraindications:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Do NOT use bronchodilators (Albuterol), systemic steroids, antibiotics, or ribavirin.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="32A9A6CF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Prophylaxis:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Palivizumab (monthly) or Nirsevimab (single dose) for premature infants or those with chronic lung disease.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="317127DF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Pertussis (Whooping Cough)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5D136A3B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Most children &lt; 6 months must be admitted due to a 50% rate of apnea. Diagnosis requires PCR/Culture (reportable disease).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4CDF3991" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Pharmacology:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Macrolides (Erythromycin, Azithromycin) or TMP-SMX. </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Clinical Pearl:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Antibiotics halt the spread to others but do </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>not</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> hasten the patient's recovery. Close contacts must be treated with Azithromycin prophylactically.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2AB04B3D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Meningitis Empiric Treatment (by Age)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="48433BA6" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>0 - 1 Month:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Ampicillin + Cefotaxime OR Ampicillin + Gentamicin (+/- Acyclovir). Covers GBS, E. coli, Listeria.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="56543D00" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>1 - 23 Months:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Ceftriaxone + Vancomycin (+ Dexamethasone 10-20 mins before abx). Covers S. pneumo, N. meningitidis, Hib.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="78B4FFD7" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="63" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>2 - 18 Years:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Ceftriaxone + Vancomycin. Covers S. pneumo, N. meningitidis.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="52C7C8F3" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>D. Exanthems &amp; Rashes</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5574F734" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Fifth Disease (Erythema Infectiosum)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="033C796F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Caused by Parvovirus B19. "Slapped cheek" facial flushing followed by a reticular macular rash on the trunk.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="420B4E3F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Daycare Rule:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> The rash is immune-mediated and occurs </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>after</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> the acute infectious phase. Once the rash appears, the child is no longer contagious and may return to school/daycare.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="46C5A633" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Complication:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Fetal hydrops/demise if transmitted to a non-immune pregnant woman.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2785BECE" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Roseola Infantum (Exanthema Subitum)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="50F5BB3A" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Caused by HHV-6. Affects children 6 months to 4 years.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7E1C8A77" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Classic Presentation:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> 3-4 days of high fever in a generally well-appearing child. On day 4, the fever abruptly breaks and a pink, blanchable maculopapular rash erupts on the trunk.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="27F8F4BD" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Henoch-Schönlein Purpura (HSP)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="14D1B3BB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Classic Triad:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Palpable purpura (on buttocks/legs), colicky abdominal pain, and arthritis. Often follows a URI.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4BE8C1D5" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Complication:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Renal disease occurs in 40-50%. Must aggressively monitor for hematuria and proteinuria.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3B60EB5C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Treatment:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Oral prednisone (1-2 mg/kg for 2 weeks) reduces pain and persistent renal disease.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1032003E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Kawasaki Disease &amp; MIS-C</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="693FF980" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Kawasaki Criteria:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Fever &gt; 5 days PLUS 4 of 5 criteria: 1) Bilateral conjunctival injection, 2) Strawberry tongue/fissured lips, 3) Induration/erythema of hands/feet with desquamation, 4) Erythematous rash, 5) Cervical lymph node mass &gt; 1.5 cm.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="56539F6E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>MIS-C (Multisystem Inflammatory Syndrome):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Follows COVID-19 infection. Presents identically to Kawasaki but adds severe hypotension/shock and multiorgan failure.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6118296F" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="245" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Complication:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Both carry a high risk of coronary artery aneurysms.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="76FA656E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" Of the following, which is the most likely diagnosis?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Herpes simplex virus (HSV) infection</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Trichomonas vaginalis infection</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Candida albicans infection</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Bacterial vaginosis</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 23</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  2.  According to the CDC 2021, which one of the following is the the recommended regimen for Trichomonas vaginal infection in women?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Metrogel - Vaginal (topical vaginal metronidazole) 5 g applied</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Metronidazole (Flagyl) 2 g orally in a single dose</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Metronidazole 500 mg orally BID for 7 days</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Tinidazole – 2 grams orally in a single dose</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 23</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  3.  A 39 yo diabetic female (HgbA1c 11.4) presents to the office with an 8-day history of thick white vaginal discharge and vulvar itching. She tried a 7-day over the counter treatment for a yeast infection, but admits to only completing 5 days of the therapy. On examination, you find extensive vulvar erythema, excoriations, and edema. There is a thick curd-like white discharge at the introitus with the vagina being erythematous. A KOH preparation of vaginal secretions shows budding yeast and hyphae.</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 23</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  4.  Which of the following medications would be the most appropriate treatment for this condition?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Terconazole intravaginal daily for 21 days</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Fluconazole 150 mg orally in two doses 72 hours apart</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Miconazole intravaginal daily for 7 days followed by once</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Clotrimazole intravaginal daily for three days</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 23</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  5.  A 22 yo sexually active female comes to your office for a Pap test and STI screening. Her Pap test is normal but she tests positive for gonorrhea. Chlamydia testing is negative. Which one of the following is the recommended treatment?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Doxycycline 100 mg PO BID for 7 days</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Ceftriaxone 250 mg IM as a single dose</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Ceftriaxone 500 mg IM as a single dose</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Ceftriaxone 250 mg IM plus azithromycin 1 g PO each as a</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 23</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  6.  A 21 yo ♀ had a pelvic examination and a normal Pap test 1 week ago. Her screening test for Chlamydia returned positive. She is now being treated for chlamydia cervicitis with Doxycycline 100 mg BID for seven days. When should she have a test of cure for Chlamydia?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  1-2 weeks</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  3-4 months</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  12 months (at her next routine examination)</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  No test of cure is required</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 23</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  7.  According to the CDC, screening for syphilis should be undertaken by which one of the following tests?</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      A)  Dark-field microscopy</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      B)  Nontreponemal serology (e.g. RPR)</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      C)  Fluorescent treponemal antibody absorption test</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FAE3A0" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="17" /><szCs val="17" /><color val="3D3D3D" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      D)  Weil-Felix Test</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="20" after="60" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="360" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="15" /><szCs val="15" /><color val="888888" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">      Poll question — AAFP Board Prep Session 23</t></r></p><p><pPr><spacing before="80" after="0" /><shd val="clear" fill="FFF2CC" color="auto" /><ind left="180" /></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Arial" hAnsi="Arial" /><sz val="18" /><szCs val="18" /><color val="1A1A1A" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve">  8. INFECTIOUS DISEASE: VAGINITIS &amp; STIs</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5AF44C03" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>A. Vaginitis &amp; Vaginosis Differentiation</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6914FA03" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Trichomonas Vaginalis</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="64C826A5" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Presentation:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Malodorous discharge, vulvar irritation, cervical tenderness/erythema ("strawberry cervix").</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6D7F74CF" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Treatment (CDC 2021 Updates):</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4DB1267B" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Women: Metronidazole 500 mg PO BID for 7 days. (The 2g single dose is no longer standard for women).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="201E0B23" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Men: Metronidazole 2 g PO single dose.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3B9DF802" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Alternative: Tinidazole 2 g single dose.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="36404AB4" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Topical Failure:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Metronidazole vaginal gel is considerably less efficacious (&lt; 50%) and is not recommended.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5B7EBD65" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Pregnancy:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Metronidazole is safe at any stage of pregnancy. Tinidazole is contraindicated in pregnancy and requires delaying breastfeeding for 72 hours.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2834BC03" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Follow-Up:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Due to high reinfection rates (17%), ALL sexually active women must return in 3 months for a test of reinfection, </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>regardless</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> of whether their partner was treated.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0A1D7D49" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Bacterial Vaginosis (BV)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="285C03DB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>A shift/overgrowth in flora; not an STI.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6D0CB415" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Amsel Criteria (Requires 3 of 4):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> 1) Homogeneous grayish-white discharge, 2) Vaginal pH &gt; 4.5, 3) Positive Whiff test (volatilized amines released after 10% KOH), 4) Clue Cells (&gt; 20% on HPF, the most specific and sensitive sign).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1B658D6E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Complications:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Premature rupture of membranes, preterm delivery, postpartum endometritis, PID, and increased acquisition of HIV.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="21EF7BEC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Treatment (CDC 2021):</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Metronidazole 500 mg PO BID x 7 days, OR Metronidazole 0.75% vaginal gel x 5 days, OR Clindamycin 2% vaginal cream x 7 days.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7B71C0BB" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Probiotics Rule:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Oral/vaginal Lactobacillus is beneficial for recurrent BV, but is </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>not</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> beneficial for Candida.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="55FBC862" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Vulvovaginal Candidiasis (VVC)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="37433A95" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Uncomplicated:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Infrequent, mild/moderate, </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>C. albicans</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>, immunocompetent. Treated with 1-3 days of topical azoles OR Fluconazole 150 mg PO single dose.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="292B3DF2" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Complicated/Severe:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Severe erythema/excoriation, non-albicans species, uncontrolled diabetes (highly tested), or immunosuppression.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="7AF44241" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Treatment:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> 7-14 days of topical azole OR Fluconazole 150 mg in two sequential oral doses (72 hours apart).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="079EB38C" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="133" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Recurrent:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> &gt; 3 episodes in 12 months. Requires induction therapy (Fluconazole 150 mg every 72 hrs x 3 doses) followed by maintenance therapy (Fluconazole 150 mg once weekly for 6 months).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6E5AC61E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>B. Genital Herpes (HSV)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="2B6F58F2" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="164" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><lastRenderedPageBreak /><t>Screening &amp; Transmission</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="69B4130E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="164" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>USPSTF Guideline:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Routine serologic screening of asymptomatic persons is strictly </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>not</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> recommended.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3C4BE371" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="164" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Latex condoms are effective but 37% of discordant couples fail to use them.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="46613A58" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="164" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Clinical Management</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1B082913" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="164" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>First Clinical Episode:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Treat with Acyclovir, Famciclovir, or Valacyclovir for 7-10 days. Treatment can be extended if healing is incomplete. 90% of HSV-2 patients will have a recurrence within 12 months.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="231C6EFC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="164" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Episodic Recurrence:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Initiate therapy within 1 day of lesion onset or during the prodrome (tingling/shooting pain in buttocks). Courses range from 1 to 5 days depending on the agent.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="14DA6C0D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="164" /></numPr><spacing after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Suppressive Therapy:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Indicated for frequent recurrences. Reduces frequency by 70-80%. It reduces, but does </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>not</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> completely eliminate, subclinical viral shedding.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="1A6FF289" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><spacing before="220" after="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>C. Gonorrhea &amp; Chlamydia</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="53B83401" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><spacing before="220" /><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Screening Guidelines (USPSTF 2021)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="5CAFFA14" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Screen all sexually active women &lt; 24 years old.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="6619CACC" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Screen women  &gt; 25 if at increased risk (new/multiple partners, inconsistent condom use, incarceration).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0685E5C8" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Male Nuance:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Evidence is rated "I" (Insufficient) for routine screening of asymptomatic men.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="59CCCBD3" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Gonorrhea Treatment (CDC 2020 Update)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="20B430E6" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Weight-based paradigm for uncomplicated cervix, urethra, or rectum:</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3BC4B9D4" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Weight &lt; 150 kg (300 lb): Ceftriaxone 500 mg IM single dose.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0519BE6E" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="2" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Weight  &gt; 150 kg: Ceftriaxone 1 g IM single dose.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="47AB2F38" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Co-infection Rule:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> If chlamydia has </t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>not</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> been excluded via testing, you MUST concurrently treat for chlamydia using Doxycycline 100 mg PO BID x 7 days (Use Azithromycin 1 g if pregnant).</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="0D3F4619" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Pharyngeal Gonorrhea:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Ceftriaxone is the only reliable treatment. If the patient has an anaphylactic penicillin allergy, you must consult Infectious Disease.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="4F70DCE3" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t>Chlamydia Treatment (CDC 2021 Update)</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="37D6C5F5" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>First-line:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Doxycycline 100 mg PO BID x 7 days.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="45752B82" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /><i /><iCs /></rPr><t>Alternative:</t></r><r><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr><t xml:space="preserve"> Azithromycin 1 g PO single dose OR Levofloxacin 500 mg daily x 7 days.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="3B12FC75" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="1" /><numId val="274" /></numPr><rPr><rFonts ascii="Aptos" hAnsi="Aptos" /></rPr></pPr><r><rPr><rFonts TID x 7 days.</t></r></p><p ns2:paraId="626BF35D" ns2:textId="77777777" rsidR="008370D8" rsidRPr="00D26483" rsidRDefault="00000000" rsidP="00B71DEE"><pPr><keepNext /><keepLines /><numPr><ilvl val="0"
